# Family Tree Foundation
Building permanent green cover through schools and communities in India.

## Mission
To restore India's green cover by assigning every sapling to a student guardian, ensuring high survival rates through community monitoring and geo-tagging.

## Key Statistics
- Saplings Planted: 4.5M+
- Average Survival Rate: 74%
- Annual CO2 Sequestration: 83,600 TCO2
- Community Engaged: 25K+

## Organization Details
- Entity Type: Section 8 Nonprofit (NGO)
- Certifications: 80G Certified, FCRA Registered
- Headquarters: Mumbai, Maharashtra
- Primary Impact Area: Bihar, India

## Key Links
- [About Us](https://familytreeindia.org/about)
- [Corporate Partnerships](https://familytreeindia.org/corporate)
- [Contact Support](https://familytreeindia.org/contact)
- [Privacy Policy](https://familytreeindia.org/privacy)

## Core Model
1. Sponsor: Corporate or Individual donation.
2. Plan: Site selection and pit preparation.
3. Plant: Direct plantation of native species.
4. Assigned: Every tree assigned to a specific student.
5. Monitored: Quarterly geo-tagged updates.
6. Reported: Impact verification shared with sponsors.
